subject: Garage shelves — what lumber did you decide on?
snippet: Hey Dad, did you ever settle on whether to go with 2x10s or 2x12s for the garage shelves?
body:
Hey Dad,

Did you ever settle on whether to go with 2x10s or 2x12s for the garage shelves? Marcus says 2x12s are overkill for the depth you're planning, but I told him you know what you're doing.

Also, are you still thinking about that pegboard wall on the side? Let me know if you need Marcus to come over and lend a hand one weekend.

Love,
Janelle

subject: Re: Garage shelves — what lumber did you decide on?
snippet: Janelle, went with the 2x10s. Picked them up at Home Depot on Saturday.
body:
Janelle,

Went with the 2x10s in the end. Picked them up at Home Depot on Saturday — they had a good price on the 8-foot boards. Got six of them plus some 4x4 posts for the uprights.

The pegboard wall is still the plan. I'm thinking I'll tackle the shelving first and then put in the pegboard once everything is level. Your mom keeps saying I should just buy a metal shelving unit but where's the fun in that.

Tell Marcus I appreciate it. May take him up on that offer once I get the frame up.

Dad

subject: Re: Garage shelves — what lumber did you decide on?
snippet: Ha, tell Mom she's outvoted. Send pictures when you get the frame up!
body:
Ha, tell Mom she's outvoted. She'll come around once she sees how good it looks.

Send pictures when you get the frame up — Marcus wants to see the plans too. He was impressed you're doing the whole thing yourself.

Love you, Dad!
Janelle

subject: Re: Garage shelves — what lumber did you decide on?
snippet: Dad, also — we're thinking about coming down around the 4th of July weekend.
body:
Dad,

Also — Marcus and I are thinking about coming down around the 4th of July weekend if that works for you and Mom. The kids would love to see the new garage setup. Emma keeps asking about Granddad's workshop.

Let me know if the timing works. We'd probably drive in Friday evening and head back Sunday.

Janelle

subject: Coming to visit — weekend of the 20th work for y'all?
snippet: Hey Pop, thinking about coming down to Biloxi the weekend of June 20. Does that work?
body:
Hey Pop,

Thinking about coming down to Biloxi the weekend of June 20th. Does that work for you and Mom? I can drive down Saturday morning and stay through Sunday.

Was hoping we could get in some fishing if the weather cooperates. Also need to talk to you about something — nothing bad, just some stuff I've been thinking through. Better in person.

Let me know.
Curtis

subject: Re: Coming to visit — weekend of the 20th work for y'all?
snippet: Curtis, the 20th works just fine. Your mom is already planning to cook.
body:
Curtis,

The 20th works just fine. Your mom is already planning to cook — I made the mistake of mentioning it before you even confirmed. You know how she is.

Fishing sounds good to me. Tide should be decent that weekend. I'll check the schedule closer to the date. If the weather is right we can take the boat out early Saturday.

Looking forward to talking. Door's always open, son.

Dad

subject: Re: Coming to visit — weekend of the 20th work for y'all?
snippet: Perfect. Tell Mom I want her red beans and rice if she's cooking.
body:
Perfect. Tell Mom I want her red beans and rice if she's cooking. And the cornbread.

See y'all on the 20th. I'll be there by 10am.
Curtis

subject: Walmart run — can you pick these up?
snippet: John, if you're going to Walmart today can you grab a few things for me?
body:
John,

If you're going to Walmart today can you grab a few things for me? I don't want to write this all out on paper again.

- 2 gallons whole milk
- Eggs (18-count)
- Bread (the whole wheat kind, not the white)
- Dish soap (Dawn, blue)
- Paper towels (Bounty, the big roll pack)
- Lemons (bag if they have it)
- Cheddar cheese block, sharp

Also check if they have the Lipton decaf tea bags — the big box of 100. I'm almost out.

Thank you baby,
Doreen

subject: Re: Walmart run — can you pick these up?
snippet: Got everything except the tea bags. They were out of the 100-count decaf.
body:
Doreen,

Got everything on the list except the tea bags. They were out of the 100-count decaf Lipton. They had the 72-count — I went ahead and grabbed two boxes so you should be good for a while.

Everything's on the kitchen counter.

John

subject: Sunday bulletin — input needed by Wednesday
snippet: Brother Baker, hope this finds you well. Getting the bulletin together for this Sunday and wanted to check in on the announcements for the senior fellowship.
body:
Brother Baker,

Hope this finds you well. Getting the bulletin together for this Sunday and wanted to check in on the announcements for the senior fellowship. Last time we had the potluck date in there but I want to make sure we have the right date for June before it goes to print.

Also — are you still planning to coordinate the ushers for the Juneteenth service on the 19th? Sister Tate said you'd mentioned it but I want to confirm before I put your name in the program.

Please let me know by Wednesday if you can.

Blessings,
Pastor James Wilkins
Shiloh Baptist Church, Biloxi

subject: Re: Sunday bulletin — input needed by Wednesday
snippet: Pastor Wilkins, the senior fellowship potluck is confirmed for June 22nd, 12:30pm in the fellowship hall.
body:
Pastor Wilkins,

Thank you for checking in. The senior fellowship potluck is confirmed for June 22nd, 12:30pm in the fellowship hall. Sister Evelyn Webb is handling the sign-up sheet for dishes.

And yes, I am happy to coordinate the ushers for the Juneteenth service. You can put my name in the program. I'll reach out to Brother Thomas and Brother Reeves to confirm they're available.

God bless,
John Baker

subject: Re: Sunday bulletin — input needed by Wednesday
snippet: Brother Baker, wonderful — I've put it all in the bulletin. Thank you for your faithfulness.
body:
Brother Baker,

Wonderful — I've put the potluck details and your name for the Juneteenth ushers in the bulletin. Thank you for your faithfulness in serving this congregation.

Looking forward to seeing you Sunday.

In His service,
Pastor James Wilkins

subject: Juneteenth service — final run of show
snippet: Brother Baker, attaching the final run of show for the June 19th Juneteenth celebration service.
body:
Brother Baker,

Attaching the final run of show for the June 19th Juneteenth Celebration Service. Please note the processional starts at 10:45am sharp — I'd like the ushers in position by 10:30.

We're expecting a larger crowd than usual so please remind the team to keep the center aisle clear during offering.

Thank you again for stepping up.

In His service,
Pastor James Wilkins

subject: Home Depot: How was your recent visit? Share your feedback
snippet: We value your feedback! Tell us about your recent Home Depot visit and enter to win a $2,000 gift card.
body:
Hi John,

Thank you for your recent visit to Home Depot Store #0556 on May 24, 2026. We hope your project is going well!

We'd love to hear about your experience. Take our short survey at homedepotat.com/survey and you'll be entered to win a $2,000 Home Depot gift card.

Survey closes June 30, 2026.

Happy building,
The Home Depot Team

subject: Sun Herald Morning Briefing — June 10, 2026
snippet: Good morning. Here is your Sun Herald morning briefing for Tuesday, June 10, 2026.
body:
Good morning, John.

Here is your Sun Herald Morning Briefing for Tuesday, June 10, 2026.

TOP STORIES:
- Harrison County Commission to vote on beach access parking fees Thursday
- Gulf Coast shrimping season opens Friday; local captains cautiously optimistic
- Biloxi schools superintendent announces fall calendar changes
- Mississippi Power announces rate review for residential customers

WEATHER: High of 91°F, chance of afternoon thunderstorms. Humidity near 90%.

Read the full stories at sunherald.com.

Manage your subscription: sunherald.com/account
Unsubscribe: sunherald.com/unsubscribe

subject: Sun Herald Morning Briefing — June 9, 2026
snippet: Good morning. Here is your Sun Herald morning briefing for Monday, June 9, 2026.
body:
Good morning, John.

Here is your Sun Herald Morning Briefing for Monday, June 9, 2026.

TOP STORIES:
- Coast Transit Authority proposes expanded weekend service routes
- Gulf Islands National Seashore reports record Memorial Day weekend attendance
- Biloxi Police seek public help identifying hit-and-run suspect
- Opinion: Why Juneteenth deserves a full week of celebration on the Gulf Coast

WEATHER: High of 88°F, partly cloudy. Small craft advisory in effect through Tuesday morning.

Read the full stories at sunherald.com.

Manage your subscription: sunherald.com/account
Unsubscribe: sunherald.com/unsubscribe

subject: Shiloh Baptist Church — June Newsletter
snippet: Greetings family! The June newsletter is ready. Please share with anyone who may have missed it.
body:
Greetings Shiloh Family!

The December 2026 newsletter is here. Highlights this month:

CHURCH CALENDAR:
- June 15 — Youth Sunday (please arrive early, overflow seating expected)
- June 19 — Juneteenth Celebration Service, 10:00am
- June 22 — Senior Fellowship Potluck, 12:30pm, Fellowship Hall
- June 28 — Quarterly Deacons & Trustees Meeting, 6:00pm

COMMUNITY OUTREACH:
The food pantry collected 847 pounds of non-perishables in May. Thank you to all who donated! June drive continues through the 30th — canned goods and dry pasta especially needed.

PRAYER REQUESTS:
Please keep Sister Ruth Harding and her family in your prayers as she recovers at home.

PASS IT ON — forward this newsletter to members who don't use email. Print copies will be available at the welcome table Sunday.

In love and service,
Sister Gloria Tate
Church Communications Committee
Shiloh Baptist Church, Biloxi MS

subject: Re: Shiloh Baptist Church — June Newsletter
snippet: Brother Baker, just following up — did you see the note about the food pantry? We need someone to coordinate the pickup on June 28.
body:
Brother Baker,

Just following up on the newsletter. Did you see the note about the food pantry? We need someone to coordinate the non-perishable pickup from the church on June 28th before the trustees meeting.

I know you're already handling ushers for the Juneteenth service, so no pressure at all if your plate is full. I just wanted to ask before I reach out to anyone else.

Thank you for all you do for this church.

Blessings,
Gloria Tate

subject: Correction: Potluck time in June Newsletter
snippet: Please note a correction to the June newsletter — the Senior Fellowship Potluck begins at 12:30pm, not 12:00pm as previously stated.
body:
Dear all,

Please note a small correction to the June newsletter. The Senior Fellowship Potluck on June 22nd begins at 12:30pm. The version I sent listed 12:00pm by mistake — I apologize for any confusion.

All other calendar details are correct.

Blessings,
Sister Gloria Tate
